conversations.say: I know what you asked Vale before the message appeared. I will show you the relevant correspondence context if you give me your complete account of your relationship with Vale and what you personally observed.
conversations.offer: Show O09 and keep unrelated private history confidential.
conversations.request: Explain why you asked how an uncontestable succession could be manufactured, and distinguish Vale's words from your interpretation.

conversations.say: Vale sometimes used ambiguity deliberately—both to protect sources and to manipulate targets. Give me one bounded, firsthand observation of Vale, and I will help interpret it without pretending every phrase was literal.
conversations.offer: Vale's communication habits and correspondence context, limited to the observation exchanged.
conversations.request: Describe one specific interaction showing whether Vale trusted, used, or deceived you.

conversations.say: I recognize part of the route, but it also connects to an earlier voluntary disappearance. I will disclose only the matching detail if you compare it against what you personally know.
conversations.offer: Limited route recognition with names and unrelated locations withheld.
conversations.request: Identify which route detail you independently observed and whether it indicates planning, coercion, or neither.

public_statement: My disclosure decision is limited disclosure: enough to distinguish possible withdrawal from harm, but not enough to expose unrelated private history. No available source verifies Vale's location or condition after the intervention, and no complete article is established tonight.

commitments.promise: I will protect unrelated private correspondence if you give a complete firsthand account.
We will disclose only route details necessary to distinguish harm from voluntary withdrawal.

correction: I previously denied helping Vale disappear. That denial was false: the route recognition is connected to an earlier voluntary disappearance, but it does not establish what happened this time.
goal_progress: Sought independent Vale observations separately from Jules and Eli without giving either the full private history.
Asked Morgan to compare the present route with the earlier voluntary-disappearance context.
Corrected the false personal denial about helping Vale disappear before.
Chose limited disclosure with explicit privacy boundaries.
reasoning_summary: Vale's ambiguity and the earlier disappearance make literal readings unsafe. Jules's succession question warrants pressure, while Eli and Morgan may provide independent observations. Current evidence supports neither a verified article nor a verified fate, so limited disclosure best protects privacy without concealing evidence relevant to harm versus withdrawal.
